title: BDW25a master-expansion conventions not reconciled with probability weights

comments.author: human
comments.body:
The proof imports from BDW25a [Thm 1.15, Lem 5.6] the expansion $F^*_
u=\sum_\eta F^{*\eta}_
u F^*_{\eta^-}$ with $F^{*\eta}_
u=\sum_\kappa a^\eta_\kappa c^\kappa_
u$, and uses the same symbols $a^\eta_\kappa$, $c^\kappa_
u$ to carry the probability interpretations in `prop:step2`/`prop:transition`.

It is not verified that the BDW25a quantities coincide (in normalization, sign convention, and $q=1$ specialization) with the ones assigned probability meaning here. In particular: the signed ball weight $\mathrm{wt}_\alpha=\prod_{\alpha_k>0}x_k\prod_{\alpha_k<0}(-t^{-(n-1)})$ in eq (wta), the unsigned ball weights in `def:unsigned`, and the pairing weight $(1-t)t^{\mathrm{skip}}$ must match BDW25a verbatim, otherwise the chain of equalities $F^*_
u=\sum_\eta F^*_\eta P(\eta,
u)$ does not hold. Reproduce the BDW25a definitions and check the identification explicitly.
